candidates.source_title: Hashima Island 2023 — from tourist vessel at sea
candidates.photographer: ノーマルエディタ (Wikimedia user)
candidates.attribution_text: ノーマルエディタ / Wikimedia Commons / CC BY-SA 4.0
candidates.legal_note: CC BY-SA ShareAlike clause: if this image is color-graded or modified for the video, the resulting work may technically require CC BY-SA licensing. Seek legal review for commercial YouTube publishing. If cleared, attribution must appear in video description.
candidates.notes: HIGHEST VISUAL MATCH for S001. Shot from a tourist vessel at sea level — exactly what the scene requires. Extreme wide, island silhouette visible. Ultra-high resolution (9248×6936) — excellent for editorial color grading. RECOMMEND for S001 pending legal review of SA clause.

candidates.source_title: Gunkanjima Island — exterior view with explorer in foreground
candidates.attribution_text: Jordy Meow / Wikimedia Commons / CC BY-SA 3.0
candidates.legal_note: CC BY-SA 3.0 ShareAlike clause applies. Same SA concern as C001-A.
candidates.notes: Shows a person (explorer/tourist) in the foreground with the island ruins behind. Person is not in a context of suffering — they appear to be a visitor/tourist. However the presence of a human figure may conflict with the dark_documentary aesthetic intended for S001 (no people, desolate). LOWER PRIORITY. Better alternatives exist.

candidates.source_title: Battle-Ship Island Nagasaki Japan — aerial exterior
candidates.legal_note: CC BY 2.0 — NO ShareAlike requirement. Safest free license for commercial YouTube. Attribution in video description is sufficient.
candidates.notes: AERIAL shot, not sea-level — minor visual mismatch for S001 which calls for sea-level perspective. However it shows the island silhouette and overall structure clearly. HIGHEST LEGAL SAFETY of free candidates. Designated 'Valued Image' on Wikimedia Commons. If SA clause is a concern for C001-A, this is the safe fallback for S001.

candidates.source_title: Hashima Island — PIXTA stock photography library
candidates.legal_note: PIXTA Royalty-Free license is explicitly cleared for commercial YouTube video production. No SA clause. 30,594+ Gunkanjima images available. Search: 軍艦島 端島 全景. License fee varies by plan.
candidates.notes: Japanese stock platform — best coverage of Hashima Island real photography. Multiple sea-level exterior shots available. RECOMMENDED for cleanest commercial license if budget allows. Specific image and price must be confirmed at purchase.

candidates.source_title: Battle-Ship Island Nagasaki Japan — aerial, battleship silhouette
candidates.legal_note: CC BY 2.0 — NO ShareAlike. Cleanest free license. Attribution in video description only.
candidates.notes: RECOMMENDED for S007. Aerial shot clearly showing the battleship silhouette — exactly what the scene requires. Designated Wikimedia Commons 'Valued Image' (December 2022). No people visible. Shot with Olympus E-510, 2008.

candidates.source_title: National Land Image Archive — Hashima aerial 1974 (year of closure)
candidates.photographer: Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism of Japan (国土地理院 — Geospatial Information Authority of Japan)
candidates.license_type: Attribution (CC-BY equivalent — National Land Image Information license)
candidates.attribution_text: Made based on National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs), Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism of Japan
candidates.legal_note: Japanese government public data. Attribution-only license, no SA. The required attribution string is long but clear. Commercial use explicitly permitted.
candidates.notes: HISTORICAL SIGNIFICANCE: This aerial photograph was taken in 1974 — the year of the island's closure. Showing the island at peak abandonment adds documentary authenticity unavailable in any other candidate. Strongly recommended as S007 alternate or S024 historical context cut.

candidates.source_title: The island of a warship — aerial view of Hashima
candidates.legal_note: CC BY 2.0 — same clean license as C007-A. Same photographer, same session.
candidates.notes: Same photographer and same day as C007-A (kntrty, December 7, 2008). Different angle/elevation. Good aerial view of island structure. Lower resolution than C007-A. Use as alternate angle if two aerial cuts needed.

candidates.legal_note: CC BY-SA 4.0 ShareAlike applies. Consult legal before using in commercially published, color-graded video.
candidates.notes: Aerial view taken December 2020. Recent and clear. Portrait orientation (1031×1200) may require cropping for 16:9 video. Lower resolution than C007-A.

candidates.source_title: Fourth mine pit hoistroom remains — Hashima 1992
candidates.legal_note: CC BY-SA 4.0 ShareAlike applies. However: S008 in image_plan.json already planned as AI_GENERATE (not the sensitive mine scene — that is S011a/IMG009). This image shows the coal mine hoistroom exterior/mechanical equipment, not the tunnel interior. No forced labor context — purely industrial machinery.
candidates.notes: RECOMMENDED for S008. Shows Fourth Mine Pit Hoistroom (第四竪坑捲座跡) — actual coal mining infrastructure on Hashima. NO people visible. Shot 1992 — 18 years after closure. Industrial atmosphere matches scene requirement. Also shows Building 65 and school gymnasium in background. This is REAL mining infrastructure, not AI-generated. IMPORTANT: This is distinct from the sensitive mine tunnel scene (S011a/IMG009) — this is an exterior shot of the hoistroom building, not a tunnel depicting forced labor context.

candidates.source_title: Hashima Dorr thickener — coal processing equipment
candidates.notes: Shows Dorr thickener (coal processing drum equipment) and conveyor belt remains. Photographed from the 7th floor of the school building. NO people. Industrial archaeology. Complements C008-A by showing processing equipment rather than shaft infrastructure. Both from same photographer, same day.

candidates.source_title: Hashima Belt Conveyor and Building 70 — industrial ruins 2017
candidates.notes: High resolution (4608×2592). Shows rusting belt conveyor infrastructure alongside former school building (No. 70). Contemporary photography (2017), showing advanced decay. NO people visible. Large format allows significant cropping. Olympus OM-D E-M5 Mark II — high quality sensor.

candidates.source_title: Getty Images — Gunkanjima / Hashima industrial ruins
candidates.legal_note: Getty RF or RM license required. Specific license tier must confirm: commercial online video use, worldwide, in perpetuity. Confirm there is no 'editorial only' restriction on chosen image.
candidates.notes: 905 Gunkanjima images on Getty. Professional editorial and creative photography available including aerial, interior, exterior, and atmospheric shots. RECOMMENDED if budget allows — cleanest legal path for commercial YouTube.

candidates.legal_note: CC BY-SA 4.0 ShareAlike applies. Very high resolution — color grading this image = modification = SA clause triggered.
candidates.notes: RECOMMENDED for S020. Wide exterior showing the island ruins with sky and concrete structures. 6000×4000 pixels — exceptional resolution for color grading / dark_documentary treatment. Shot with Sony α6300 at 50mm f/7.1. No people visible. Contemplative wide framing aligns with ACT IV pacing requirement.
Second shot from same photographer and session as C020-A. Provides visual variety from the same location. Use alongside C020-A if multiple cuts of S020 are needed. No people visible.

candidates.source_title: Battle-Ship Island Nagasaki Japan — aerial for ACT IV context
candidates.legal_note: CC BY 2.0 — cleanest free license. No ShareAlike. Can be color-graded without SA concern.
candidates.notes: BEST LEGAL SAFETY for S020 among free candidates. Aerial view. Less ideal for the 'contemplative' ACT IV tone (aerial feels more dynamic than reflective), but safe to use commercially with attribution only. Could pair with slow_zoom_out motion to create contemplative mood.

candidates.legal_note: PIXTA RF license cleared for commercial video. No SA clause. Best option for ACT IV if budget allows — search for dusk/atmospheric shots.
candidates.notes: 30,594+ Gunkanjima images. For S020, search keywords: 軍艦島 夕暮れ / 端島 夕焼け / Gunkanjima dusk / Hashima atmosphere. Filter for horizontal (landscape) orientation. RECOMMENDED paid option.

candidates.source_title: Hashima Island 2023 — from tourist vessel, final wide shot candidate
candidates.photographer: ノーマルエディタ (Wikimedia user)
candidates.attribution_text: ノーマルエディタ / Wikimedia Commons / CC BY-SA 4.0
candidates.legal_note: Same CC BY-SA 4.0 as C001-A. If used for both S001 and S024, a single legal clearance covers both uses.
candidates.notes: RECOMMENDED for S024 (same source as C001-A for S001). Ultra-high resolution allows two distinct crops for two different shots — wider establishing frame for S001 and tighter silhouette crop for S024 final shot. This is the most practical approach: one license clearance, two scenes covered. NOTE: S024 is the FINAL shot of the entire video. The image captures the island from the sea, exactly as intended. Emotional resonance is high.

candidates.source_title: National Land Image Archive — Hashima aerial 1974 (year of closure)
candidates.photographer: Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism of Japan (国土地理院)
candidates.license_type: Attribution (CC-BY equivalent — National Land Image Information license)
candidates.attribution_text: Made based on National Land Image Information (Color Aerial Photographs), Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism of Japan
candidates.legal_note: Attribution-only, no SA clause. Safest free license. Government public data for commercial use.
candidates.notes: Alternative for S024 with highest historical resonance: this aerial shows the island in the ACTUAL year of closure (1974). Using it as the final shot creates a powerful time-loop: we begin in the present (S001) and end in 1974 — the year everything stopped. Strong editorial choice if approved by director. Resolution moderate (1900×2100) — sufficient for 1920×1080 output.

candidates.photographer: Unknown (source: Japanese geography book 'Series of Japanese geography and folk culture: Vol.13', Shinkosha, pre-1947)
candidates.legal_note: Public domain — zero restrictions. Published before January 1, 1957 under Japanese copyright law.
candidates.notes: LEGAL SAFETY PERFECT (Public Domain). However: resolution is 522×248 pixels — FAR too low for 1920×1080 video production. Would require 3.7× upscaling to fill frame — significant quality degradation even with AI upscaling. USE ONLY as brief historical insert (1-2 seconds) with film-grain texture applied to lean into the archival quality. NOT recommended as primary S024 final shot.

getty_images.notes: Confirm 'online video / YouTube' use type at checkout. Editorial images are NOT cleared for commercial use.

pixta.notes: Japanese platform, best coverage for Japanese historic sites. Search: 軍艦島, 端島, 軍艦島 廃墟, 端島 海.

alamy.notes: Confirm use type includes online video. RM licenses must be negotiated per use.
